blocked.abettor: abettor is the standard (legal) form in both AmE and BrE; abetter is the less-standard variant
blocked.adapter: both valid internationally; adapter dominates in software (Adapter pattern, network adapter) - converting corrupts dev docs
blocked.advisement: vocabulary swap - advisement and advice are different lexemes
blocked.advisements: vocabulary swap; target advisings is also not a real word
blocked.advisors: advisor is valid international English with split usage vs adviser
blocked.ampul: target ampoul is not a word; correct BrE is ampoule (broken target)
blocked.ampuls: target ampouls is not a word; correct BrE is ampoules (broken target)
blocked.bandana: bandana is common everywhere, not distinctly American; split usage
blocked.bicyclist: vocabulary swap - drops bi, different word from cyclist
blocked.burglarization: derivational/lexeme change to burglary, not a spelling variant
blocked.burglarizer: target burgler is a misspelling of burglar (broken target)
blocked.burglarizers: target burglers is a misspelling of burglars (broken target)
blocked.burka: transliteration variant (burka/burqa/burkha), not an AmE/BrE spelling difference; both used in BrE
blocked.bused: both bused and bussed are accepted internationally; converting either way is noise
blocked.busing: both busing and bussing are accepted internationally; converting either way is noise
blocked.centennial: vocabulary swap - centennial/centenary are different words, centennial is used in BrE
blocked.cesia: botanical Latin species epithet (caesia); fixed taxonomic orthography, conversion risks corrupting scientific names
blocked.chamomile: chamomile is common/standard in BrE too; split usage
blocked.cloture: parliamentary term (ending debate), distinct from closure - conversion corrupts meaning
blocked.coliseum: many venues are proper-noun Coliseum (London Coliseum, Oakland Coliseum); Colosseum is a different proper noun
blocked.collectible: collectible is extremely common in BrE; split usage
blocked.coloration: coloration is the OED headword and standard in scientific BrE (BrE drops the u before -ation); conversion unnecessary
blocked.curb: curb = restrain (curb spending) is standard everywhere and common; homograph with road-edge sense
blocked.czar: czar is valid international English (data czar etc.); split usage with tsar
blocked.defecate: defecate is standard modern BrE; defaecate is a dated variant
blocked.defensed: target defenced is not a standard English word (AmE sports verbing of defense)
blocked.defensing: target defencing is not a standard word; risks confusion with fencing
blocked.dependents: dependents = reverse dependencies is extremely common in dev docs (npm, build graphs); dependants means people
blocked.diked: dike/dyke both valid internationally; dyked also carries slang risk
blocked.ethereal: ethereal is standard in both AmE and BrE; aethereal is archaic
blocked.extemalization: OCR-garbled entry - source is broken (should be externalization) and target exstemalisation is nonsense
blocked.fagot: target faggot is an offensive slur - dangerous for an automatic converter despite the legit bundle/stitch sense
blocked.fetida: botanical Latin epithet (foetida, e.g. Passiflora); taxonomic
blocked.fetidissima: botanical Latin epithet (foetidissima, e.g. Iris); taxonomic
blocked.fetidus: botanical Latin epithet (foetidus, e.g. Helleborus); taxonomic
blocked.fjord: fjord is standard everywhere including BrE; fiord is NZ-only
blocked.fliers: flier/flyer split usage in both AmE and BrE, not a clean AmE->BrE mapping
blocked.gluings: gluing is the standard/preferred spelling in both AmE and BrE; glueing is the less-standard variant
blocked.gobbledygook: gobbledygook is OED-primary and common in BrE; gobbledegook is the variant
blocked.gotten: grammatical/dialectal swap, not spelling; also breaks ill-gotten -> ill-got
blocked.grody: different slang lexeme - grody (AmE) vs grotty (BrE) are distinct words
blocked.guerrilla: guerrilla is the standard spelling everywhere; guerilla is the less-correct variant
blocked.hankie: hankie/hanky are informal variants used everywhere, not AmE/BrE
blocked.hippie: hippie is standard everywhere; hippy is also an adjective meaning big-hipped (homograph)
blocked.impanelled: m2e standardises on impanelled; adding impanelled->empanelled would chain conversions
blocked.impanelling: m2e standardises on impanelling; adding impanelling->empanelling would chain conversions
blocked.imposter: imposter/impostor both used in AmE and BrE; not an AmE/BrE distinction
blocked.kindergartner: both kindergartner and kindergartener are American; neither is British (kindergarten not used in BrE)
blocked.licensure: vocabulary swap: licensure is a distinct US term, not a spelling of licensing
blocked.lissome: lissome and lissom both valid in BrE; split usage
blocked.math: technical collision: Math class/namespace, math package/module, math.h - corrupts code
blocked.meow: meow is standard international English; miaow is a dated/regional variant
blocked.meowed: meow is standard international English; miaow is dated/regional
blocked.meowing: meow is standard international English; miaow is dated/regional
blocked.meows: meow is standard international English; miaow is dated/regional
blocked.methemoglobinemia: target methaemoglobinemia is a non-standard hybrid (should be methaemoglobinaemia)
blocked.mnesarchella: target mnesarchaella corrupts the genus name Mnesarchella (which has no ae)
blocked.moggy: both are British spellings; moggy is the common one, not an Americanism
blocked.moma: not standard American; collides with proper noun MoMA
blocked.momma: target mumma is dialect, not standard British (mum/mummy are the standard forms)
blocked.mommas: target mummas is dialect, not standard British
blocked.mommys: target mummys is an incorrect plural (should be mummies)
blocked.myna: myna is the standard ornithological spelling; mynah is a variant, not a US->UK difference
blocked.mynas: broken mapping: plural mynas mapped to singular mynah
blocked.naught: naught means nothing in international English (all for naught); nought is the digit - corrupts idiom
blocked.neoarchean: garbled target naeoarchean; correct international form is neoarchaean
blocked.neuron: neuron is standard international/British (neural networks, neuroscience); neurone is dated - damaging in ML/AI docs
blocked.neurons: neuron is standard international/British; neurone is dated - damaging in ML/AI docs
blocked.normalcies: derivational word-form swap (-cy vs -ity), not spelling
blocked.normalcy: normalcy->normality is a derivational word-form swap (-cy vs -ity), not a spelling variant
blocked.numskull: numskull is a standard international spelling; numbskull is a variant, not US->UK
blocked.numskulls: numskull is standard international; numbskull is a variant, not US->UK
blocked.obligating: different lexeme; obliging also means accommodating/helpful
blocked.obligatingly: obligingly (helpfully) is a different word and meaning
blocked.obligator: obligor is a distinct legal term, not a spelling of obligator
blocked.obligators: obligors is a distinct legal term
blocked.oversize: oversize and oversized are both valid; grammatical form change, not spelling
blocked.palaestra: reversed direction: palaestra (ae) is the British/classical form; strips ae to American palestra
blocked.palaestras: reversed direction: strips ae from the British/classical form
blocked.patine: vocabulary swap to a different verb patinate; patine is not a standard American spelling
blocked.pedagogical: pedagogical is standard modern British; paedagogical is archaic
blocked.pedagogically: pedagogical is standard modern British; paedagogical is archaic
blocked.pedagogicals: pedagogical is standard modern British; paedagogical is archaic
blocked.pederast: paederast is archaic; modern British English uses pederast
blocked.pederasts: paederasts is archaic; modern British English uses pederasts
blocked.persons: different lexeme; persons is valid formal/legal English (missing persons, persons of interest)
blocked.physiologic: -ic vs -ical derivational form change, not a spelling variant
blocked.physiologics: -ic vs -ical derivational form change, not a spelling variant
blocked.plunk: different word; British plonk also means cheap wine, plunk is to drop/strum
blocked.plunker: plonker is British slang for a fool; different word (Plunker/plnkr is also a dev tool)
blocked.presidium: praesidium is a dated variant; modern British English uses presidium
blocked.presidiums: praesidiums is a dated variant; modern British English uses presidiums
blocked.shivaree: etymological/vocabulary swap to French charivari, not a spelling normalisation
blocked.snuck: grammatical past-tense variant, not spelling; snuck is widely accepted internationally
blocked.tabor: tabor is the standard spelling; tabour is archaic; also proper noun (Mount Tabor)
blocked.tabors: tabor is the standard spelling; tabour is archaic; proper-noun risk
blocked.thruway: proper-noun collision (e.g. New York State Thruway); throughway is rare
blocked.unbeknownst: unbeknownst is standard in both AmE and BrE; unbeknown is the less common older form
blocked.updraft: updraft is standard international (aviation/meteorology); updraught is dated; split usage
blocked.updrafts: updraft is standard international; updraught is dated; split usage
blocked.veranda: veranda is standard British; verandah is a regional variant
blocked.verandas: veranda is standard British; verandah is a regional variant
blocked.volunteerism: different word from volunteering (the practice/policy vs the act)
